verdict: pass

## Verdict: PASS (post-dominates n1)
0 BLOCKER / 0 MAJOR. Walkthrough re-run by reviewer: exit 0, "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed".

## Acceptance criteria — all met
1. Re-claim succeeds: clearAdvisoryClaim lists repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{n}/comments, matches
   `<!-- kw:claim project=<project> -->`, deletes via repo-level endpoint repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{id}. Verified.
2. Other project still blocks: match is project-scoped; trailing ` -->` prevents prefix collision (issue-92 != issue-920). Verified.
3. Offline unchanged: OFFLINE short-circuit precedes every new gh call; returns skipped_offline. Verified.

## Robustness / contract
- New gh block wrapped in try/catch; JSON.parse + for-of guarded; per-comment DELETE independently try-guarded. Network/parse failure cannot crash finalize/discard.
- Return contract removed/failed/skipped_offline preserved; existing #163 tests pass.
- All 4 call sites thread correct project: cmdFinalize→args.project, discard→folder.project, pr-merged→folder.project, pr-closed→folder.project.

## Byte-identity & scope
- scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js == plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js (diff clean).
- Only the 3 declared files changed.

## Findings (non-blocking)
- MINOR: null-`project` fallback regex would delete EVERY project's marker — UNREACHABLE today (all callers pass truthy project). Recommended follow-up: make delete-nothing the default when project is unknown.
- NIT: gh api comments first-page only (~30); marker posted early so practically a non-issue.
